signal.raw_content:
OFFICE OF THE UNITED STATES TRADE REPRESENTATIVE

Federal Register Vol. 91, No. 127 — Section 301 Tariff Action: Pharmaceutical Starting Materials and API Intermediates

FINAL RULE — Effective August 1, 2026

PURSUANT to Section 301 of the Trade Act of 1974, USTR is implementing a 55% ad valorem tariff on pharmaceutical starting materials and API intermediates under HTSUS Chapters 28 and 29 originating from the People's Republic of China.

This final rule supersedes the proposed rule published May 5, 2026 (FR Doc. 2026-09876). After review of 847 public comments, USTR has maintained the 55% rate without modification.

COVERED PRODUCTS include but are not limited to: aromatic nitro compounds and their reduced derivatives (HTS 2921.49), amide coupling reagents including benzotriazole-based uronium salts (HTS 2934.99), anhydrous polar aprotic solvents (HTS 2921.59), and protected amine intermediates for pharmaceutical synthesis (HTS 2924.29).

EFFECTIVE DATE: August 1, 2026. No grace period for goods in transit as of the effective date.

ESTIMATED COST IMPACT: The US pharmaceutical industry currently imports approximately $4.2 billion annually in materials covered by this action. For manufacturers with greater than 80% China-origin exposure on key starting materials, cost per kg API is estimated to increase by 20-30% depending on tariff-exposed material fraction.

COUNTERMEASURES: No exclusion process is available at this time. USTR notes that manufacturers may petition for exclusions no sooner than 90 days after the effective date.

Contact: frnotices@ustr.eop.gov.
